text: You are using AI effectively from a client-facing perspective, but your failure to integrate it into internal operations may cause clients to be skeptical of your commitment to the technology, and your lower focus on employee education and holistic health risks losing engagement and commitment

You are deploying AI to run your business effectively but you risk unsustainability of you do not better prepare your employees  for an AI-enabled workplace. In addition, finding ways to deploy AI for client-facing activities can demonstrate your commitment to growth more effectively than maintaining AI as an internal-only tool

You are providing your employees with conceptual information on AI, but you are making little use of AI in your actual business operations, and you aren't actually deploying it to make your employee's lives better. The longer you wait, the farther you fall behind

You are focusing on your employees' holistic health, which is admirable, but if you aren't education your employees about AI, they will still feel anxiety about their role in the future workforce, and if you don't employe AI in your business operations, you risk falling behind
You are focusing on your employees' holistic health and educating them on how to use it which is admirable, but if you don't employe AI in your business operations, you risk falling behind

You are using AI effectively from a client-facing perspective and educating your employes effectively, but you send mixed signals by not employing it in internal operations and by ignoring its potential to improve employee holistic health. Ultimately, your AI effort may fail if you don't even your focus

You are using AI effectively from a client-facing perspective, but the lack of employee education and failure to integrate it into back office operations may cause clients to be skeptical of your commitment to the technology

You are deploying AI to run your business effectively and paying attention to employee holistic health, but failing to deploy it for client facing operations or to educate your workforce marks your company as a laggard both internally and externally

You are providing your employees with conceptual information on AI and paying attention to employee holistic health, but you are making little use of AI in your actual business operations. The longer you wait, the farther you fall behind
You are focused on deploying AI but are relatively less focused on employee holistic health. Employees remain an important part of business and deploying AI to keep them well should be a priority.

You are deploying AI for everything but your internal operations, which sends a confusing mixed message to your employees and to your clients, who may be skeptical of your true commitment to the technology.
You are deploying AI to solve many problems, internally and externally, but if you don't focus more on employee education, your efforts may fall flat.
You are deploying AI for everything but client-facing work. This will mark you as a laggard in the market, no matter how much you do internally.

You are deploying AI to run your business effectively and doing an admirable job preparing your employees for an AI-enabled workplace, but not deploying AI for client-facing services can mark your company as a laggard
